data.title: Baytex Energy (TSX:BTE) EPS Slide In Q3 2025 Tests Bullish Efficiency Narrative
data.description:
Baytex Energy (TSX:BTE) has turned in a mixed set of FY 2025 numbers, with Q3 revenue at $746.4 million and EPS of $0.04, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of $3.1 billion and EPS of $0.28 that still leave the company in loss making territory over the past year. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from $850.8 million in Q3 2024 to $746.4 million in Q3 2025, while EPS shifted from $0.23 to $0.04 over the same period. This has set up a results season in which investors are weighing thin profitability against the broader earnings trajectory. With production costs per barrel, realized commodity prices and margins all in close focus, this earnings print puts operating efficiency at the center of the story.

See our full analysis for Baytex Energy.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up with the most widely held stories about Baytex, and where the data pushes back on those expectations.

See what the community is saying about Baytex Energy

Advertisement

Production Holds Near 13.7 MMboe While Pricing Softens

  • Total oil equivalent production in Q3 2025 came in at 13.74 million barrels of oil equivalent, close to Q2 2025 at 13.48 million and Q3 2024 at 14.06 million. The realized unhedged gas price was $2.62 per unit versus $3.16 in Q2 2025 and $3.99 in Q1 2025.
  • Analysts' consensus view highlights efficiency gains in areas like Eagle Ford and Pembina Duvernay as a key support for production and margins. At the same time, flat production around 13 to 14 million barrels of oil equivalent and softening gas prices mean any efficiency improvement has to work harder just to keep revenue and earnings steady.

Unit Costs Steady Around $11.50 Per BOE

  • Average production cost per barrel of oil equivalent in Q3 2025 was $11.54, very close to $11.95 in Q2 2025, $11.38 in Q1 2025 and $11.76 in Q3 2024. This points to relatively consistent unit operating costs through different pricing conditions.
  • Supporters of the bullish narrative point to plans for heavy oil developments and expansion in Clearwater and Mannville as production drivers. This relatively tight band of production costs suggests the near term earnings impact depends heavily on realized prices rather than a clear break lower in cost per barrel.
🐂 Baytex Energy Bull Case

Unprofitable Today With Forecast Earnings Swing

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Baytex shows a net loss of $276.8 million in Q4 2025 with basic EPS of $0.36 loss. Forecasts in the data call for earnings growth described as very large over the next three years, even as revenue is expected to decline about 4.5% per year.
  • Bears focus on the company still being unprofitable over the last 12 months and an unstable dividend record. This concern lines up with the reported trailing loss and falling trailing revenue from $3.3b in Q1 2025 to $1.5b in Q4 2025, even though the same forecast set calls for a very large improvement in earnings from here.
🐻 Baytex Energy Bear Case

data.title: Is Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) A Potential Opportunity After Recent Share Price Weakness
data.description:

  • The stock closed at US$56.48 recently, with returns of 1.0% over the last 30 days but declines of 12.3% year to date and 11.9% over the past year, which hints at shifting expectations around its prospects and risk profile.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Skyworks Solutions Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For Skyworks Solutions, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections in US$. The latest twelve month free cash flow (FCF) is about $1.11b. Analysts have provided FCF estimates out to 2030, with Simply Wall St extrapolating further years using modest step downs in growth rates. The 10 year projections, which range around the mid to high $800m to just under $1b mark each year, are discounted to reflect the time value of money.

Bringing all those projected and discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of US$66.39 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$56.48, the DCF implies Skyworks is trading at a 14.9% discount to this intrinsic estimate, which points to the shares looking undervalued on this model.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Skyworks Solutions is undervalued by 14.9%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 47 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Skyworks Solutions.

Approach 2: Skyworks Solutions Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like Skyworks Solutions, the P/E ratio is a useful yardstick because it directly links what you pay today to the earnings the business is currently generating. It gives you a quick sense of how many dollars investors are willing to pay for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a reasonable P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth potential and risk profile. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk often goes with a higher P/E, while slower expected growth or higher risk tends to line up with a lower multiple.

Skyworks is trading on a P/E of 21.54x. That sits below the Semiconductor industry average P/E of 42.05x and also below the peer group average of 35.77x. On simple comparisons, this might suggest a lower valuation than many sector names.

Approach 1: Fiserv Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit Fiserv is expected to generate over and above the return that shareholders require, then adds that stream of excess value to the company’s book value per share.

For Fiserv, the starting Book Value is US$48.30 per share, with a Stable EPS estimate of US$10.79 per share, based on weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 8 analysts. The implied Cost of Equity is US$5.86 per share, which leaves an Excess Return of US$4.93 per share. That excess is driven by an Average Return on Equity of 16.23% applied to a Stable Book Value of US$66.48 per share, which is based on future book value estimates from 4 analysts.

Putting these inputs through the Excess Returns framework results in an estimated intrinsic value of about US$157.62 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$63.86, the model suggests Fiserv trades at a 59.5% discount. On this approach, the stock screens as materially undervalued.

P/E is a useful yardstick for a profitable business like Fiserv because it ties the share price directly to current earnings, which you can observe today rather than estimate far into the future.

In general, investors tend to accept a higher or lower P/E depending on what they expect for future earnings growth and how risky they think those earnings are. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while lower growth expectations or higher risk usually align with a lower P/E.

Fiserv is currently trading on a P/E of 9.81x. That sits below the Diversified Financial industry average of 18.62x and also below the peer average of 32.02x. data.title: Bank Of America Weighs AI Bubble Risks And Future Tech Debt Issuance
data.description:
• Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) released new research on investment-grade credit investors focused on artificial intelligence related risks.
  • The survey shows more investors now flag a potential AI bubble as their top concern versus AI driven corporate obsolescence.
  • Respondents also expect major AI companies to issue more debt in 2026, with possible implications for credit markets and sector risk.

For you as an investor, this research from Bank of America, NYSE:BAC, sits at the intersection of AI enthusiasm and credit markets. The bank is a major participant in global lending and capital markets, so its read on how investment-grade credit investors think about AI risk helps frame where caution and opportunity may be building. In the context of elevated AI related spending, credit investors are focusing more on how that spending is financed and what it means for balance sheets.

Expectations of higher debt issuance by major AI players in 2026 could influence how you think about sector exposure, credit quality, and concentration risk. If borrowing accelerates, questions around the sustainability of current AI driven valuations and the trade off between equity and debt financing may become more important in portfolio decisions.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Bank of America by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Bank of America.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Bank of America following this latest news.

For Bank of America, the AI sentiment survey lands alongside very active balance sheet management. In early March 2026 the bank announced several new fixed income offerings in US dollars and other currencies, with coupons such as 5.05% notes due 2038 and 4.10% notes due 2029, as well as zero coupon notes due 2038. At the same time, it is calling multiple senior notes early across US$, euro and yen tranches, including US$2.8b of 1.658% notes due 2027 and €1.75b of floating rate notes due 2027. For you, that mix of issuance and redemptions shows the institution actively refreshing its funding stack while also positioning for what it sees in credit markets, including potential AI related issuance by clients. The research on an AI bubble and increased tech borrowing can help Bank of America tailor underwriting standards, pricing and sector limits as it helps large AI names come to market. For equity holders, this speaks to how the bank may earn fee and interest income from AI linked financing, while still needing to manage its own funding costs and exposure if sentiment toward AI sectors swings.

How This Fits Into The Bank of America Narrative

  • Active issuance and redemption activity across currencies fits with the narrative that disciplined interest rate and asset management can support future net interest income and earnings power over time.
  • If AI related credit conditions tighten or funding costs remain elevated, that could challenge expectations for smooth balance sheet growth and expense discipline that underpin the longer term story.
  • The survey focus on an AI bubble and higher debt issuance by large tech names is not fully captured in the existing narrative, yet it could influence future loan growth, fee income and credit risk that investors may want to factor into their own view.

  • ⚠️ Ongoing refinancing of its own senior notes at coupons such as 4% to 5% may keep funding costs higher than legacy low coupon debt, which can pressure net interest margins if asset yields do not keep pace.

What To Watch Going Forward

data.title: Mazda Motor Weighs Record North America Sales Against Global Weakness
data.description:
• Mazda Motor reported record February sales in North America, with strong volumes in both the U.S. and Canada.
  • The sales strength comes shortly after reports of double digit global sales declines earlier in the year.
  • Investors are watching how this regional resilience may relate to Mazda Motor's current share price of ¥1,199.5 on TSE:7261.

Mazda Motor, traded as TSE:7261, is drawing fresh attention after setting a record for February sales in North America, even as earlier data pointed to double digit global sales declines. The stock is currently at ¥1,199.5, with a 1 year return of 23.5% and a 5 year return of 58.8%, which indicates how the market has viewed the company over different time frames. In the shorter term, the share price has seen a 7 day decline of 13.6% and a 30 day decline of 2.5%.

The contrast between record North American sales and softer global figures may reflect how Mazda is leaning on core markets while responding to weaker conditions elsewhere. As new data on unit volumes, pricing, and regional mix emerge, investors can reassess how this combination of regional strength and global headwinds fits into their view of Mazda Motor at its current valuation and recent performance.

📰 Beyond the headline: 4 risks and 2 things going right for Mazda Motor that every investor should see.

The latest North American sales update suggests Mazda Motor is finding traction in its key crossover offerings even as global volumes have recently been softer. In the U.S., February deliveries of 33,497 vehicles were flat year on year in absolute terms, but up 4% on a daily selling rate basis, with CX-50 internal combustion and hybrid models achieving their best February on record. In Canada, 4,616 vehicles were sold, up 1.3% and the highest February in more than a decade, helped by strong demand across the CX-5, CX-30, CX-70, and CX-90 lineup. For investors, that points to a product mix in North America that currently resonates with buyers, particularly in crossovers, a segment where Mazda faces heavy competition from Toyota, Honda and Hyundai.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ January 2026 global sales were 10% lower year on year at 91,024 units, with domestic Japan, overseas and especially China all weaker. This shows that Mazda’s strength is not broad based across all regions.
  • ⚠️ Analysts flag four key risks for Mazda Motor, including a dividend that is not well covered by earnings or free cash flows and profit margins of 0.2% that are lower than 2.7% last year. This means headline volume numbers alone do not capture the full financial picture.
  • 🎁 North American crossovers like CX-50, CX-5, CX-30, CX-70 and CX-90 are achieving record or very strong February results. This could support Mazda’s market position in higher value segments if that demand is sustained.
  • 🎁 Rewards data points to two key positives that investors are watching, including expectations for earnings growth and indications that the shares may be trading slightly below some fair value estimates.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it will help to track whether the strong February performance in the U.S. and Canada is reflected in Mazda Motor’s upcoming quarterly revenue and margin figures, especially given the recent 10% global sales decline in January and the sharper drop in China. Investors may also want to watch how Mazda balances pricing and incentives on its CX crossover range, and how its product mix in North America compares with peers such as Toyota’s RAV4 and Highlander, or Honda’s CR-V and Pilot. Any commentary from management on regional demand trends, inventory levels and the outlook for the dividend coverage could be important for those weighing the recent share price pullback against the company’s current fundamentals.

Approach 1: Grupo Aeroméxico. de Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to a present value.

For Grupo Aeroméxico. de, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. For a company that is generating earnings, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to see what investors are currently paying for each dollar of profit. It condenses a lot of expectations into a single number and is easy to compare with other companies in the same space.

What counts as a "normal" or "fair" P/E depends on what the market expects for growth and how risky the earnings stream appears. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk usually lines up with a lower multiple.
